title: Bordo
content:

{{Quote|Take the controls, Weeg. I'll teach them some manners.|Kyle Katarn prepares to open fire on Bordo and the Grave Tuskens|<ref name="RA" />}}
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]], '''Bordo''' was one many Tuskens to be recruited by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] affiliated [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]]. Originally from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Bordo was a follower of the Tusken [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]]. When Hett was unmasked by the Jedi [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], Hett's protégé [[KkH'Oar'Rrhr]] led Hett's followers offworld. Once discovered by Maw, they were transformed into a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] gang known as the [[Grave Tuskens]], and were used to rob ancient Jedi graves. 

KkH'Oar'Rrhr eventually left the gang, and the Tusken [[Rogg]] took his place as the leader of the Tusken band, acting under Maw. Around [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]<ref name="Date">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' is set in 5 ABY, mentions that Bordo was second for "three long years".</ref> Bordo became Rogg's second-in-command, a relationship that proved strenuous. Bordo grew to hate his leader and sought to take his place. Rogg had a high sense of survival and wished to persuade his band that he should have nominal, and safer, leadership, but Bordo blocked the discussion.<ref name="RA" />

During the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], the Tuskens were on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] with their forces scattered around the [[Katarn family compound]] and the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Barons Hed]], with Rogg's band making their home in a rest stop along the highway. When [[Kyle Katarn]] [[Mission to Sulon|returned to his home]] and commandeered a [[T-4 (vehicle)|T-4 troop transport]] towards Barons Hed, Rogg's band of Grave Tuskens gave chase on their swoop bikes.<ref name="RA" />

During the course of the chase, the transport entered a bridge towards the [[city/Legends|city]]. Rogg veered to the right with some of his followers dodging Kyle Katarn's fire from the transport's [[Turret/Legends|turret]]; Bordo led the rest of the team to the left side of the bridge; three Tuskens were already shot down, bringing big disappointment to Bordo that Rogg was still alive.<ref name="RA" />

Bordo [[Death/Legends|shot]] Rogg, took brief control of the gang, boarded Kyle's T-4, and was subsequently shot off of the transport by Kyle. The Jedi escaped, and a number of the remaining Tuskens slammed into an Imperial blockade.

==Biography==
===Birth of the Grave Tuskens===
[[File:Maw-JKDFII.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Maw, master of the Grave Tuskens]]
Born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Bordo was a [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] [[Gender/Legends|male]],<ref name="RA" /> and a member of the tribe of the Tusken-raised [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]].<ref name="Blog" /> Sometime during or after the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="TNEC_AotC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']] to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> Bordo learned of the [[Mission to rescue Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|massacre]] of the [[Rrhr hunting band]], although he was unaware of the culprit:<ref name="Blog" /> [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]].<ref name="AotC">{{Film|II}}</ref> One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], Bordo's companion, [[KkH'Oar'Rrhr]]—also known as Hoar—entered the [[Ben Kenobi's home/Legends|home]] of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] hermit [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], and found the Jedi's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] weapon. Activating it, Hoar experienced a vision of a masked lightsaber wielder striking Hoar down. When Kenobi returned to his hut, Hoar fled, and later told Bordo and the Tusken [[Sliven]] about the vision. Both Tuskens told Hoar that the being in the vision must have been the Rrhr clan's [[Death/Legends|killer]].<ref name="Blog" />

During the year [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]],<ref name="LaLoOWK">''[[The Life and Legend of Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'' dates the [[Duel on Tatooine (Imperial Period)|duel]] between [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]] to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]].</ref> Hett's clan attacked the [[Lars homestead/Legends|homestead]] of a man named [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]]. However, they were intercepted by Kenobi, who [[Duel on Tatooine (Imperial Period)|battled]] Hett. Kenobi won, and tore off Hett's face covering bandages. As having one's face uncovered was an act of shame according to Tusken standards, many of Hett's followers abandoned him.<ref name="Blog" />

The Tusken Jedi ended up leaving Tatooine behind. Hoar, Bordo, and a multitude of others did not give up on Hett so easily. Hett was pursued by those who still admired the fallen tribesman. The Tuskens' search brought them to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], and they visited planets such as [[Portug]] and [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]]. While on Dromund Kaas, however, they encountered a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] follower of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|emperor]], [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. The [[Boltrunian/Legends|Boltrunian's]] name was [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]], and the Dark Jedi took the rogue Tuskens under his wing, turning them into a [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]]. To honor Hett, the new gang of Tuskens adorned new masks that bore to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] their bare faces.<ref name="Blog" />
[[File:Tython-TOR2.png|thumb|The planet Tython, where Grave Tuskens earned their name of tomb robbing.]]
After robbing Jedi tombs on the planet [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], Maw officially named his new henchman the [[Grave Tuskens]]. Eventually, the Imperial agent [[Arden Lyn]] discovered Maw's followers, and took Hoar away to train in the combat art of [[Teräs Käsi/Legends|Teräs Käsi]].<ref name="Blog" /> During [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]],<ref name="TNEC_Rogg">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. The text establishes that [[Rogg]] had been in command of the [[Grave Tuskens]] for three years. Using simple math, the start of his command can be pinpointed at [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]].</ref> the Tusken [[Rogg]] became the gang's leader, while Bordo fell in as Rogg's second-in-command.<ref name="RA" />

===Second to none===
During [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="TNEC_RotJ">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> the Empire had been dealt a serious blow with the death of Emperor Palpatine.<ref name="RotJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> [[5 ABY/Legends|One year]] later,<ref name="TNEC" /> the Dark Jedi [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] rose from the ashes. Maw had since become a member of Jerec's cabal,<ref name="RA" /> and naturally, the Grave Tuskens followed in his footsteps.<ref name="RA" />

Despite holding the rank of second-in-command, Bordo nonetheless chafed under Rogg's leadership and grew resentful of his superior. At some point, Rogg sought to survive longer by leading his gang from a safer place instead of leading attacks from the front. Bordo was one who cried out against this decision.<ref name="RA" />

Eventually, the Tuskens were sent to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] where they acted as "enforcers", patrolling their vicinity; their base was in a rest stop along the highway between [[Barons Hed]] and the [[farm/Legends|farmlands]]. Jerec had tasked his forces with the raiding of a [[Katarn estate|farm]] that belonged to [[Morgan Katarn]], a man Jerec had previously murdered. The [[Katarn family compound|farmhouse]] held a [[Map to the Valley of the Jedi|map]] to the mythical [[Valley of the Jedi]] embedded in its ceiling, which Jerec valued greatly. Grave Tuskens were stationed all about Katarn's home, as well as the nearby [[City/Legends|city]] [[Barons Hed]].<ref name="RA" />

Shortly after the map had been recovered by Jerec's forces, Katarn's son, [[Kyle Katarn|Kyle]], [[Mission to Sulon|returned to his home]], also looking for clues about the Valley of the Jedi, as well as Jerec who murdered his father. Katarn found and reactivated the family [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[WeeGee]], and the two followed the traces of the stolen Map to [[Barons Hed]]. For this, they hijacked a [[T-4 (vehicle)|T-4 speeder]], but not before alerting the Imperials with their activities. With that, Rogg's gang were alarmed and gave chase to the fleeing son of a farmer on their [[speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]]. Kyle used the gun on the speeder to shoot at the Tuskens, with Rogg evading the bolts, his survival always angering Bordo.<ref name="RA" />

Eventually, the chase led to a bridge above a canyon; the band was divided to right and left with Bordo flying up on the T-4's left side; setting his bike on auto, he managed to board alongside another Tusken. Bordo felt that it was time to usurp control of the gang from Rogg, and he shot the Tusken leader in the face, killing him, before having the time to board. Bordo was confident that nobody saw his act amid the chaos of the battle. Meanwhile, the T-4 ended up in the shadow of Kyle's [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]''. As Kyle communicated with the [[Jan Ors|pilot]], Bordo and Kyle simultaneously raised their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] at each other. However, the man, who was a fledgling Jedi himself, proved faster with the trigger than Bordo, and shot the Grave Tusken through the chest. Bordo died, and his body fell over the speeder's tailgate and onto the road. The second Tusken was also shot and killed by the Jedi. Kyle and WeeGee were extracted from the speeder by the ship, which then slammed into an Imperial blockade. Several of the other Tuskens, who had been going way to fast to slow down, also crashed into the barricade and were killed.<ref name="RA" />

==Personality and traits==
When Hett was unmasked by Kenobi after a fierce duel, many Tuskens abandoned their leader due to the shame associated with being unmasked. However, Bordo was one in a handful of Tuskens that continued to look up to Hett. Following Hoar, Bordo ran among the stars in search of Hett, but instead found a new master: the Dark Jedi Maw.<ref name="Blog" /> Later yet, the Tusken Rogg came to act as the leader of the Tuskens<ref name="RA" /> under Maw.<ref name="Blog" /> However, Bordo found Rogg's command to be a grating experience, and grew tired of his leader's command. Since Rogg wanted to lead his men from a safer position, Bordo believed Rogg to be a coward, and spoke out against this change.<ref name="RA" />

Three years of Rogg's command and obeying, answering and apologizing to him was enough for Bordo; he felt that his continued compliance to Rogg and the chief's self-important attitude was enough to choke a [[Krayt dragon/Legends|krayt dragon]]. During the speeder chase on Sulon, Bordo became angry when three of their men killed and Rogg surviving. After boarding Kyle Katarn's speeder, Bordo's first order of business was to turn around and shoot his commander, and act that caused him to smile. Before moving on to engage Kyle, Bordo made sure that his murder was lost in the confusion of the chase.<ref name="RA" />

==Equipment==
Like the other Grave Tuskens, Bordo operated a swoop bike. The Grave Tuskens' bikes normally stood vertically when not in motion. The bikes were equipped with [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]]. Bordo also carried a blaster on his person.<ref name="RA" /> Although many of the Grave Tuskens took to wearing masks that revealed their skin as an honor towards Hett,<ref name="Blog" /> Bordo still covered his face in bandages.<ref name="RA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Bordo was created for the [[1998]] novella ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'', written by [[William C. Dietz]]. Although the Tuskens in the novella are not named Grave Tuskens,<ref name="RA" /> the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] that the novella is based upon, ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'', establishes the name of the Tusken group affiliated with the Empire.<ref name="DFII" /> Bordo and the other Tuskens do not appear in the [[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|audio drama]] based on the novella.<ref name="RA:Audio">[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]</ref> Bordo was later mentioned again in the second part of the [[Star Wars Blog]] article, ''[[Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire]]'', which explained the history of the Grave Tuskens.<ref name="Blog" />

==Appearances==
